title: Python Execution, Tools, and Evidence · Behavior Case: Python Repl Script
summary: Build and diagnose a small command-line learning log across REPL, script, editor, and terminal workflows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the python repl script increment in a reproducible command-line learning log. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose REPL, script, module, or notebook execution based on the task and reproducibility needs.
Explain how source, the interpreter, objects, and standard streams participate when a Python program runs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
